REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANTS MELI BROWN, GWENDOLYN BROWN, AND PHILIP GRAY'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFFS EXPERT WITNESS DEPOSITIONS IS DENIED. MOVING PARTY PROVIDES NO AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT THE REQUESTED RELIEF OF COMPELLING PLAINTIFF'S EXPERT DEPOSITION BASED ON UNTIMELY AND INSUFFICIENT NOTICE OF DEPOSITION IS AVAILABLE. PLAINTIFF'S RELIANCE ON THE TWO CASES CITED IN THE POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IS MISPLACED. NEITHER CASES IS FACTUALLY ON POINT; NEITHER ADDRESSES NEGLECT TO TIMELY SERVE DISCOVERY REQUESTS; NEITHER STANDS FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT FAILURE TO PROPOUND TIMELY DISCOVERY RESULTS IN "JUDGMENT, DISMISSAL, ORDER, OR OTHER PROCEEDING TAKEN AGAINST HIM OR HER," A BASIS FOR CCP 473(B) RELIEF. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC3.1312(B). JUDGE RONALD E. QUIDACHAY, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED (D501)

Off Calendar Nov-26-2018 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Feb-26-19 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Expert Witness Depositions
Off Calendar Nov-20-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs MELI BROWN, GWENDOLYN BROWN, and PHILIP GRAY's Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Expert Witness Depositions is DENIED. Moving party provides no authority for the proposition that the requested relief of compelling Plaintiff's expert deposition based on untimely and insufficient Notice of Deposition is available. Plaintiff's reliance on the two cases cited in the Points and Authorities is misplaced. Neither cases is factually on point; neither addresses neglect to timely serve discovery requests; neither stands for the proposition that failure to propound timely discovery results in "judgment, dismissal, order, or other proceeding taken against him or her," a basis for CCP 473(b) relief. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported (D501)

Notice Of Motion To Reopen Discovery For The Purpose Of Expert Witness Depositions
Off Calendar Nov-20-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs MELI BROWN, GWENDOLYN BROWN, and PHILIP GRAY's Motion To Reopen Discovery For The Purpose Of Expert Witness Depositions is DENIED. The trial in this action is on November 26, 2018. Meet and Confer declaration required by CCP 2024.050(a) is limited to one conclusory statement, which in no way states "facts showing a reasonable and good faith attempt at an informal resolution." CCP 2016.040. Re-opening discovery on the eve of trial is prejudicial to Plaintiff. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Ronald E. Quidachay, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported (D501)

Motion For Order Permitting Discovery Of The Financial Condition Of Defts The Estate Of Michael L. Brown, Meli Brown, Gwendolyn Brown, And Philip Gray; Memo Of P&A
Off Calendar Nov-13-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances on TUE-NOV-13-2018:
Roger D. Yuen, Esq. of Hartsuyker, Stratman & Williams-Abrego (T: 510.457.3478), attorney for defendants / opposing parties;
No other appearances.

The tentative ruling is adopted as follows: PLAINTIFFS ANTONAE ROBERTSON, DOMINC MOORE, AND AALIYAH PERRY'S Motion For Order Permitting Discovery Of The Financial Condition Of Defendants The Estate Of Michael L. Brown, Meli Brown, Gwendolyn Brown, and Philip Gray.
Plaintiffs' motion is denied. Plaintiffs have not made a showing sufficient to establish a substantial probability that plaintiffs will establish by clear and convincing evidence that any defendant has been guilty of oppression, fraud or malice. Order signed in open court.

Judge Pro Tem: Roger Mead; Clerk: L. Fong; Proceedings not reported. (302/JPT)
